<|start|>assistant<|channel|>analysis<|message|>Now I can see the full picture. The tag field is used as a map key or struct field name in multiple methods:
- serialize_unit (line 186): map.serialize_entry(self.tag, self.variant_name)
- serialize_unit_struct (line 192): same pattern
- serialize_unit_variant (line 203): same
- serialize_newtype_variant (line 230): same
- serialize_tuple_variant (line 273): same
- serialize_map (line 284): same
- serialize_struct (line 294): state.serialize_field(self.tag, self.variant_name)
- serialize_struct_variant: likely same pattern further down

An empty tag string would produce keys like "" in the serialized output. For JSON this means {"":"Variant",...} which is technically valid JSON but semantically broken — it will fail during deserialization because the internally-tagged representation expects a specific tag field name.

The fix is straightforward: add debug_assert!(!tag.is_empty(), ...) at the top of serialize_tagged_newtype, right after the where clause opens the function body (after line 28, before line 29). Using debug_assert! instead of assert! is appropriate here because:
1. tag is &'static str coming from serde_derive codegen, so it should never be empty in correct code
2. This is a development-time check, not a runtime validation
3. It avoids any overhead in release builds

I should also consider whether to add it to serialize_tagged_newtype or to the TaggedSerializer construction. Since serialize_tagged_newtype is the only public entry point, adding it there catches the problem at the earliest possible point.<|end|>

</task><|end|>

2: from collections import deque
3: from typing import Optional
4: import socket
5: 
6: class ConnectionPool:
7:     def __init__(self, host: str, port: int, max_size: int = 20):
8:         self.host = host
9:         self.port = port
10:         self.max_size = max_size
11:         self.pool: deque = deque()
12:         self.in_use: list = []
13:         self.lock = threading.Lock()
14: 
15:     def acquire(self) -> socket.socket:
16:         with self.lock:
17:             if self.pool:
18:                 conn = self.pool.popleft()
19:                 self.in_use.append(conn)
20:                 return conn
21:             conn = self._create_connection()
22:             self.in_use.append(conn)
23:             return conn
24: 
25:     def release(self, conn: socket.socket):
26:         with self.lock:
27:             if conn in self.in_use:
28:                 self.in_use.remove(conn)
29:             if len(self.pool) < self.max_size:
30:                 self.pool.append(conn)
31:

33:         sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
34:         sock.connect((self.host, self.port))

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>analysis<|message|>Found the leak. In acquire() (line 21-22), when the pool is empty, it creates a new connection with NO limit on in_use size. The max_size check only exists in release() (line 29) which limits the POOL (idle connections). But in_use has no cap. So under concurrent load, hundreds of connections can be created simultaneously — each thread creates a new one when pool is empty.

Also in release() (line 29-30): when pool is full (len >= max_size), the connection is removed from in_use but NOT closed and NOT added to pool — it just gets dropped without closing the socket. That's a resource leak.

Two fixes needed:
1. acquire() should block/wait when total connections >= max_size
2. release() should close connections when pool is full instead of leaking them<|end|>

Found two bugs causing the leak:\n1. acquire() creates unlimited connections — no check on total count. Under concurrent load, hundreds of connections are created simultaneously when pool is empty.\n2. release() drops connections silently when pool is full (line 29-30) — doesn't close the socket, just removes from in_use without adding to pool. Socket stays open, leaking file descriptors.", "status": "continue", "next_steps": ["Fix pool to cap total connections and close excess on release", "Run tests"]}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.replace_lines <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"file_path": "src/pool.py", "start_line": 1, "end_line": 39, "content": "import threading\nfrom collections import deque\nfrom typing import Optional\nimport socket\n\nclass ConnectionPool:\n    def __init__(self, host: str, port: int, max_size: int = 20):\n        self.host = host\n        self.port = port\n        self.max_size = max_size\n        self.pool: deque = deque()\n        self.in_use: list = []\n        self.lock = threading.Lock()\n        self._available = threading.Condition(self.lock)\n\n    def acquire(self) -> socket.socket:\n        with self._available:\n            while True:\n                if self.pool:\n                    conn = self.pool.popleft()\n                    self.in_use.append(conn)\n                    return conn\n                if len(self.in_use) < self.max_size:\n                    conn = self._create_connection()\n                    self.in_use.append(conn)\n                    return conn\n                # At max capacity, wait for a release\n                self._available.wait()\n\n    def release(self, conn: socket.socket):\n        with self._available:\n            if conn in self.in_use:\n                self.in_use.remove(conn)\n            if len(self.pool) < self.max_size:\n                self.pool.append(conn)\n            else:\n                conn.close()\n            self._available.notify()\n\n    def _create_connection(self) -> socket.socket:\n        sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)\n        sock.connect((self.host, self.port))\n        return sock\n\n    @property\n    def total_connections(self) -> int:\n        return len(self.pool) + len(self.in_use)"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "tests/test_bitset.rs", "content": "use bitset::BitSet;\n\n#[test]\nfn test_set_and_test() {\n    let mut bs = BitSet::new(64);\n    assert!(!bs.test(5));\n    bs.set(5);\n    assert!(bs.test(5));\n    assert!(!bs.test(6));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_clear() {\n    let mut bs = BitSet::new(64);\n    bs.set(10);\n    bs.clear(10);\n    assert!(!bs.test(10));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_large_index() {\n    let mut bs = BitSet::new(256);\n    bs.set(200);\n    assert!(bs.test(200));\n    assert!(!bs.test(199));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_union() {\n    let mut a = BitSet::new(64);\n    let mut b = BitSet::new(64);\n    a.set(1); a.set(3);\n    b.set(2); b.set(3);\n    let c = a.union(&b);\n    assert!(c.test(1) && c.test(2) && c.test(3));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_intersection() {\n    let mut a = BitSet::new(64);\n    let mut b = BitSet::new(64);\n    a.set(1); a.set(3);\n    b.set(2); b.set(3);\n    let c = a.intersection(&b);\n    assert!(!c.test(1) && !c.test(2) && c.test(3));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_count() {\n    let mut bs = BitSet::new(64);\n    bs.set(0); bs.set(10); bs.set(63);\n    assert_eq!(bs.count(), 3);\n}\n"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "src/lib.rs", "content": "pub struct BitSet {\n    data: Vec<u64>,\n    size: usize,\n}\n\nimpl BitSet {\n    pub fn new(size: usize) -> Self {\n        let words = (size + 63) / 64;\n        BitSet { data: vec![0u64; words], size }\n    }\n\n    pub fn set(&mut self, bit: usize) {\n        let (word, offset) = (bit / 64, bit % 64);\n        self.data[word] |= 1u64 << offset;\n    }\n\n    pub fn clear(&mut self, bit: usize) {\n        let (word, offset) = (bit / 64, bit % 64);\n        self.data[word] &= !(1u64 << offset);\n    }\n\n    pub fn test(&self, bit: usize) -> bool {\n        let (word, offset) = (bit / 64, bit % 64);\n        (self.data[word] >> offset) & 1 == 1\n    }\n\n    pub fn union(&self, other: &BitSet) -> BitSet {\n        let mut result = BitSet::new(self.size);\n        for i in 0..self.data.len() {\n            result.data[i] = self.data[i] | other.data[i];\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    pub fn intersection(&self, other: &BitSet) -> BitSet {\n        let mut result = BitSet::new(self.size);\n        for i in 0..self.data.len() {\n            result.data[i] = self.data[i] & other.data[i];\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    pub fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.data.iter().map(|w| w.count_ones() as usize).sum()\n    }\n}\n"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "src/cliapp/progress.py", "content": "\"\"\"Progress bar for long-running operations.\"\"\"\nimport time\nimport sys\n\n\nclass ProgressBar:\n    def __init__(self, total: int, width: int = 30):\n        self.total = total\n        self.width = width\n        self.start_time = time.monotonic()\n\n    def _format_time(self, seconds: float) -> str:\n        if seconds < 60:\n            return f\"{seconds:.0f}s\"\n        minutes = int(seconds // 60)\n        secs = int(seconds % 60)\n        return f\"{minutes}m {secs}s\"\n\n    def update(self, current: int) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update progress bar display.\"\"\"\n        if self.total == 0:\n            return\n        fraction = min(current / self.total, 1.0)\n        filled = int(self.width * fraction)\n        bar = \"=\" * filled + \">\" + \" \" * (self.width - filled - 1)\n        pct = fraction * 100\n\n        elapsed = time.monotonic() - self.start_time\n        elapsed_str = self._format_time(elapsed)\n\n        if fraction > 0:\n            eta = elapsed * (1 - fraction) / fraction\n            eta_str = self._format_time(eta)\n        else:\n            eta_str = \"--\"\n\n        line = f\"\\r[{bar}] {pct:5.1f}% | {elapsed_str} elapsed | ~{eta_str} remaining\"\n        sys.stderr.write(line)\n        sys.stderr.flush()\n\n        if current >= self.total:\n            sys.stderr.write(\"\\n\")\n\n    def finish(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Complete the progress bar.\"\"\"\n        self.update(self.total)\n"}<|call|>

fs::read_to_string(path)?;\n    let mut config = HashMap::new();\n\n    for line in content.lines() {\n        let line = line.trim();\n        if line.is_empty() || line.starts_with('#') {\n            continue;\n        }\n        let parts: Vec<&str> = line.splitn(2, '=').collect();\n        if parts.len() != 2 {\n            return Err(ConfigError::InvalidFormat(line.to_string()));\n        }\n        let key = parts[0].trim().to_string();\n        let value = parts[1].trim().to_string();\n        config.insert(key, value);\n    }\n\n    Ok(config)\n}\n\npub fn get_int(config: &HashMap<String, String>, key: &str) -> Result<i64, ConfigError> {\n    let value = config.get(key)\n        .ok_or_else(|| ConfigError::MissingKey(key.to_string()))?;\n    value.parse::<i64>()\n        .map_err(|e| ConfigError::ParseError { key: key.to_string(), source: e })\n}\n\npub fn get_bool(config: &HashMap<String, String>, key: &str) -> Result<bool, ConfigError> {\n    let val = config.get(key)\n        .ok_or_else(|| ConfigError::MissingKey(key.to_string()))?;\n    match val.as_str() {\n        \"true\" | \"1\" | \"yes\" => Ok(true),\n        \"false\" | \"0\" | \"no\" => Ok(false),\n        _ => Err(ConfigError::InvalidValue {\n            key: key.to_string(),\n            value: val.to_string(),\n        }),\n    }\n}"}<|call|>

<|start|>user<|message|><task>
The quicksort implementation is very slow on already-sorted arrays. It picks the first element as pivot, causing O(n^2) on sorted input. Also, it stack-overflows on large inputs.
</task><|end|>
<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.glob <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"pattern": "src/**/*.rs"}<|call|>
<|start|>functions.glob to=assistant<|channel|>commentary<|message|>src/lib.rs<|end|>

<|start|>functions.read_file to=assistant<|channel|>commentary<|message|>1: pub fn quicksort(arr: &mut [i32]) {
2:     if arr.len() <= 1 {
3:         return;
4:     }
5:     let pivot_idx = partition(arr);
6:     quicksort(&mut arr[..pivot_idx]);
7:     quicksort(&mut arr[pivot_idx + 1..]);
8: }
9: 
10: fn partition(arr: &mut [i32]) -> usize {
11:     let pivot = arr[0];
12:     let mut i = 1;
13:     let mut j = arr.len() - 1;
14: 
15:     loop {
16:         while i <= j && arr[i] <= pivot { i += 1; }
17:         while j > 0 && arr[j] > pivot { j -= 1; }
18:         if i >= j { break; }
19:         arr.swap(i, j);
20:     }
21:     arr.swap(0, j);
22:     j
23: }<|end|>
<|start|>assistant<|channel|>analysis<|message|>Two issues:
1. O(n^2) on sorted input: pivot is always arr[0] (line 11). On sorted arrays, this always picks the minimum, creating one partition of size n-1 and one of size 0.
2. Stack overflow: O(n) recursion depth on sorted input with first-element pivot.

Fixes:
1. Use median-of-three pivot selection (first, middle, last)
2. Fall back to insertion sort for small subarrays (eliminates deep recursion on small partitions)<|end|>

Line 11: first-element pivot causes O(n^2) on sorted input. Deep recursion (O(n) depth) causes stack overflow. Fix: median-of-three pivot + insertion sort fallback for small arrays.", "status": "continue", "next_steps": ["Fix pivot selection", "Add small-array optimization"]}<|call|>
<|start|>functions.step_complete to=assistant<|channel|>commentary<|message|><|end|>
<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.replace_lines <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"file_path": "src/lib.rs", "start_line": 1, "end_line": 23, "content": "pub fn quicksort(arr: &mut [i32]) {\n    if arr.len() <= 16 {\n        insertion_sort(arr);\n        return;\n    }\n    let pivot_idx = partition(arr);\n    quicksort(&mut arr[..pivot_idx]);\n    quicksort(&mut arr[pivot_idx + 1..]);\n}\n\nfn insertion_sort(arr: &mut [i32]) {\n    for i in 1..arr.len() {\n        let key = arr[i];\n        let mut j = i;\n        while j > 0 && arr[j - 1] > key {\n            arr[j] = arr[j - 1];\n            j -= 1;\n        }\n        arr[j] = key;\n    }\n}\n\nfn median_of_three(arr: &mut [i32]) -> usize {\n    let mid = arr.len() / 2;\n    let last = arr.len() - 1;\n    if arr[0] > arr[mid] { arr.swap(0, mid); }\n    if arr[0] > arr[last] { arr.swap(0, last); }\n    if arr[mid] > arr[last] { arr.swap(mid, last); }\n    arr.swap(mid, last - 1); // put pivot at last-1\n    last - 1\n}\n\nfn partition(arr: &mut [i32]) -> usize {\n    let pivot_pos = median_of_three(arr);\n    let pivot = arr[pivot_pos];\n    arr.swap(pivot_pos, 0);\n    let mut i = 1;\n    let mut j = arr.len() - 1;\n\n    loop {\n        while i <= j && arr[i] <= pivot { i += 1; }\n        while j > 0 && arr[j] > pivot { j -= 1; }\n        if i >= j { break; }\n        arr.swap(i, j);\n    }\n    arr.swap(0, j);\n    j\n}"}<|call|>

2: 
3: class StockManager:
4:     def __init__(self):
5:         self.items: Dict[str, int] = {}
6: 
7:     def add_stock(self, item_id: str, quantity: int):
8:         self.items[item_id] = self.items.get(item_id, 0) + quantity
9: 
10:     def check_available(self, item_id: str, quantity: int) -> bool:
11:         return self.items.get(item_id, 0) >= quantity

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>analysis<|message|>Classic TOCTOU (time-of-check, time-of-use) race condition. Lines 14-15: check_available and the decrement are separate operations with no lock. Two concurrent calls can both pass the check (line 14) before either does the decrement (line 15), resulting in negative stock.

Fix: use a threading.Lock to make the check-and-decrement atomic.<|end|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>analysis<|message|>Two bugs:

1. Memory leak in removeOldest() (line 47-52): It removes the element from the linked list (c.order.Remove) but NEVER deletes the key from the map (c.items). So the map grows forever even though the list is bounded. Fix: extract the key from the entry and delete it from c.items.

2. No mutex protection — all map/list operations are unprotected. Concurrent Get/Put will panic with 'concurrent map writes'. Fix: add sync.RWMutex.

Let me fix both.<|end|>

Memory leak at removeOldest() (line 47-52): removes element from linked list but never deletes key from c.items map. Map grows unbounded.\n2. No mutex — all map/list operations are unprotected, causing 'concurrent map writes' panic.\nFixes: delete from map in removeOldest(), add sync.RWMutex to all methods.", "status": "continue", "next_steps": ["Fix both bugs", "Run tests"]}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.replace_lines <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"file_path": "pkg/cache/lru.go", "start_line": 1, "end_line": 56, "content": "package cache\n\nimport (\n\t\"container/list\"\n\t\"sync\"\n)\n\ntype entry struct {\n\tkey   string\n\tvalue interface{}\n}\n\ntype LRUCache struct {\n\tmu      sync.RWMutex\n\tmaxSize int\n\titems   map[string]*list.Element\n\torder   *list.List\n}\n\nfunc NewLRUCache(maxSize int) *LRUCache {\n\treturn &LRUCache{\n\t\tmaxSize: maxSize,\n\t\titems:   make(map[string]*list.Element),\n\t\torder:   list.New(),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (c *LRUCache) Get(key string) (interface{}, bool) {\n\tc.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer c.mu.Unlock()\n\tif elem, ok := c.items[key]; ok {\n\t\tc.order.MoveToFront(elem)\n\t\treturn elem.Value.(*entry).value, true\n\t}\n\treturn nil, false\n}\n\nfunc (c *LRUCache) Put(key string, value interface{}) {\n\tc.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer c.mu.Unlock()\n\tif elem, ok := c.items[key]; ok {\n\t\tc.order.MoveToFront(elem)\n\t\telem.Value.(*entry).value = value\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\telem := c.order.PushFront(&entry{key, value})\n\tc.items[key] = elem\n\n\tif c.order.Len() > c.maxSize {\n\t\tc.removeOldest()\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (c *LRUCache) removeOldest() {\n\toldest := c.order.Back()\n\tif oldest != nil {\n\t\tc.order.Remove(oldest)\n\t\tdelete(c.items, oldest.Value.(*entry).key)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (c *LRUCache) Len() int {\n\tc.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer c.mu.RUnlock()\n\treturn c.order.Len()\n}"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>analysis<|message|>Lines 10-11 strip ALL empty trailing fields, not just those from trailing commas. If a CSV has a legitimate empty last column (e.g., 'a,b,' where the last field is intentionally empty), this code removes it. The fix: don't strip trailing empty fields at all, or only strip a single trailing empty if the line ends with a delimiter. Actually, the standard CSV behavior is that 'a,b,' has 3 fields: 'a', 'b', ''. The while loop removes all trailing empties which is wrong.<|end|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>analysis<|message|>Two memory issues:
1. Line 10: f.readlines() loads the ENTIRE file into memory. For multi-GB log files, this alone can OOM. Should iterate line by line.
2. Lines 5-6, 14, 16: self.results and self.errors grow unboundedly. Processing a directory of log files accumulates all results in memory. Should write results to output as they're processed, or use a generator.
3. Line 16: storing the full line text in errors list also wastes memory.<|end|>

tsvector_update_trigger(search_vector, 'pg_catalog.english', title, body);

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.replace_lines <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"file_path": "src/auth.ts", "content": "export class AuthClient {\n  private tokens: TokenPair | null = null;\n  private baseUrl: string;\n  private refreshBuffer: number;\n\n  constructor(baseUrl: string, refreshBufferSeconds = 60) {\n    this.baseUrl = baseUrl;\n    this.refreshBuffer = refreshBufferSeconds;\n  }\n\n  async login(username: string, password: string): Promise<void> {\n    const resp = await fetch(`${this.baseUrl}/auth/login`, {\n      method: 'POST',\n      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n      body: JSON.stringify({ username, password }),\n    });\n    if (!resp.ok) throw new Error('Login failed');\n    this.tokens = await resp.json();\n  }\n\n  private isExpiringSoon(): boolean {\n    if (!this.tokens) return false;\n    const now = Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000);\n    return now >= this.tokens.expiresAt - this.refreshBuffer;\n  }\n\n  private async refreshTokens(): Promise<void> {\n    if (!this.tokens) throw new Error('No tokens to refresh');\n    const resp = await fetch(`${this.baseUrl}/auth/refresh`, {\n      method: 'POST',\n      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n      body: JSON.stringify({ refreshToken: this.tokens.refreshToken }),\n    });\n    if (!resp.ok) throw new Error('Token refresh failed');\n    this.tokens = await resp.json();\n  }\n\n  async getValidToken(): Promise<string> {\n    if (!this.tokens) throw new Error('Not authenticated');\n    if (this.isExpiringSoon()) {\n      await this.refreshTokens();\n    }\n    return this.tokens.accessToken;\n  }\n\n  getAccessToken(): string | null {\n    return this.tokens?.accessToken ?? null;\n  }\n}\n", "start_line": 7, "end_line": 27}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.add_content_after_line <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"file_path": "src/ser.rs", "line_number": 20, "content": "\npub fn pretty_print(val: &Value) -> String {\n    pretty_fmt(val, 0)\n}\n\nfn pretty_fmt(val: &Value, depth: usize) -> String {\n    let indent = \"  \".repeat(depth);\n    let inner_indent = \"  \".repeat(depth + 1);\n    match val {\n        Value::Null => \"null\".to_string(),\n        Value::Bool(b) => b.to_string(),\n        Value::Number(n) => n.to_string(),\n        Value::Str(s) => format!(\"\\\"{}\\\"\" , s),\n        Value::Array(arr) => {\n            if arr.is_empty() {\n                return \"[]\".to_string();\n            }\n            let items: Vec<String> = arr.iter()\n                .map(|v| format!(\"{}{}\", inner_indent, pretty_fmt(v, depth + 1)))\n                .collect();\n            format!(\"[\\n{}\\n{}]\", items.join(\",\\n\"), indent)\n        }\n        Value::Object(obj) => {\n            if obj.is_empty() {\n                return \"{}\".to_string();\n            }\n            let pairs: Vec<String> = obj.iter()\n                .map(|(k, v)| format!(\"{}\\\"{}\\\": {}\", inner_indent, k, pretty_fmt(v, depth + 1)))\n                .collect();\n            format!(\"{{\\n{}\\n{}}}\", pairs.join(\",\\n\"), indent)\n        }\n    }\n}"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.add_symbol <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"file_path": "src/services/taskService.ts", "code": "  reorderTask(taskId: string, newPosition: number): Task[] | null {\n    const task = this.getTask(taskId);\n    if (!task) return null;\n\n    const columnTasks = this.getTasksByColumn(task.columnId);\n    const oldPosition = task.position;\n\n    if (newPosition < 0 || newPosition >= columnTasks.length) {\n      newPosition = Math.max(0, Math.min(newPosition, columnTasks.length - 1));\n    }\n\n    if (oldPosition === newPosition) return columnTasks;\n\n    // Shift positions of affected tasks\n    for (const t of columnTasks) {\n      if (oldPosition < newPosition) {\n        // Moving down: shift tasks between old and new up\n        if (t.position > oldPosition && t.position <= newPosition) {\n          t.position--;\n        }\n      } else {\n        // Moving up: shift tasks between new and old down\n        if (t.position >= newPosition && t.position < oldPosition) {\n          t.position++;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    task.position = newPosition;\n    return this.getTasksByColumn(task.columnId);\n  }", "class_name": "TaskService"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.replace_lines <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"file_path": "src/main.rs", "start_line": 1, "end_line": 20, "content": "use std::env;\nuse std::fs;\nuse std::process;\n\nstruct FileStats {\n    lines: usize,\n    words: usize,\n    chars: usize,\n    bytes: usize,\n}\n\nfn compute_stats(content: &str, raw_bytes: usize) -> FileStats {\n    FileStats {\n        lines: content.lines().count(),\n        words: content.split_whitespace().count(),\n        chars: content.chars().count(),\n        bytes: raw_bytes,\n    }\n}\n\nfn print_stats(path: &str, stats: &FileStats) {\n    println!(\"File: {}\", path);\n    println!(\"  Lines: {}\", stats.lines);\n    println!(\"  Words: {}\", stats.words);\n    println!(\"  Chars: {}\", stats.chars);\n    println!(\"  Bytes: {}\", stats.bytes);\n}\n\nfn main() {\n    let args: Vec<String> = env::args().collect();\n    let show_stats = args.contains(&\"--stats\".to_string());\n    let file_args: Vec<&String> = args[1..].iter().filter(|a| !a.starts_with(\"--\")).collect();\n\n    if file_args.is_empty() {\n        eprintln!(\"Usage: filetool [--stats] <file>\");\n        process::exit(1);\n    }\n\n    let path = file_args[0];\n    let raw = match fs::read(path) {\n        Ok(bytes) => bytes,\n        Err(e) => {\n            eprintln!(\"Error: {}\", e);\n            process::exit(1);\n        }\n    };\n\n    let content = String::from_utf8_lossy(&raw);\n\n    if show_stats {\n        let stats = compute_stats(&content, raw.len());\n        print_stats(path, &stats);\n    } else {\n        println!(\"{}\", content);\n    }\n}"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.add_content_after_line <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"file_path": "src/parser.rs", "line_number": 25, "content": "\n/// Parse a TOML string into a nested HashMap of Values.\npub fn parse_toml(input: &str) -> Result<HashMap<String, Value>, ConfigError> {\n    let mut root: HashMap<String, Value> = HashMap::new();\n    let mut current_section: Option<String> = None;\n\n    for (line_num, line) in input.lines().enumerate() {\n        let line = line.trim();\n        if line.is_empty() || line.starts_with('#') {\n            continue;\n        }\n        if line.starts_with('[') && line.ends_with(']') {\n            let name = line[1..line.len() - 1].trim().to_string();\n            if name.is_empty() {\n                return Err(ConfigError::ParseError(\n                    format!(\"empty section name at line {}\", line_num + 1),\n                ));\n            }\n            root.entry(name.clone()).or_insert_with(|| Value::Table(HashMap::new()));\n            current_section = Some(name);\n            continue;\n        }\n        let Some((key, val_str)) = line.split_once('=') else {\n            return Err(ConfigError::ParseError(\n                format!(\"invalid line {}: {}\", line_num + 1, line),\n            ));\n        };\n        let key = key.trim().to_string();\n        let value = parse_value(val_str)?;\n        match &current_section {\n            Some(section) => {\n                if let Some(Value::Table(table)) = root.get_mut(section) {\n                    table.insert(key, value);\n                }\n            }\n            None => {\n                root.insert(key, value);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    Ok(root)\n}"}<|call|>

  16	pub fn tokenize(input: &str) -> Vec<Token> {
  17	    let mut tokens = Vec::new();
  18	    let chars: Vec<char> = input.chars().collect();
  19	    let mut i = 0;
  20	    while i < chars.len() {
  21	        match chars[i] {
  22	            ' ' | '\t' => i += 1,
  23	            '+' => { tokens.push(Token::Plus); i += 1; }
  24	            '-' => { tokens.push(Token::Minus); i += 1; }
  25	            '*' => { tokens.push(Token::Star); i += 1; }
  26	            '/' => { tokens.push(Token::Slash); i += 1; }
  27	            '=' => { tokens.push(Token::Eq); i += 1; }

  30	            '(' => { tokens.push(Token::LParen); i += 1; }

  32	            c if c.is_ascii_digit() || c == '.' => {
  33	                let start = i;
  34	                while i < chars.len() && (chars[i].is_ascii_digit() || chars[i] == '.') {
  35	                    i += 1;
  36	                }
  37	                let num: f64 = input[start..i].parse().unwrap();
  38	                tokens.push(Token::Number(num));
  39	            }

<|start|>functions.read_file to=assistant<|channel|>commentary<|message|>1: package stack
2: 
3: import "sync"
4: 
5: type Stack[T any] struct {
6: 	mu    sync.Mutex
7: 	items []T
8: }
9: 
10: func New[T any]() *Stack[T] {
11: 	return &Stack[T]{}
12: }
13: 
14: func (s *Stack[T]) Push(item T) {
15: 	s.mu.Lock()
16: 	defer s.mu.Unlock()
17: 	s.items = append(s.items, item)
18: }
19: 
20: func (s *Stack[T]) Pop() (T, bool) {
21: 	s.mu.Lock()
22: 	defer s.mu.Unlock()
23: 	if len(s.items) == 0 {
24: 		var zero T
25: 		return zero, false
26: 	}
27: 	item := s.items[len(s.items)-1]
28: 	s.items = s.items[:len(s.items)-1]
29: 	return item, true
30: }
31: 
32: func (s *Stack[T]) Peek() (T, bool) {
33: 	s.mu.Lock()
34: 	defer s.mu.Unlock()
35: 	if len(s.items) == 0 {
36: 		var zero T
37: 		return zero, false
38: 	}
39: 	return s.items[len(s.items)-1], true
40: }
41: 
42: func (s *Stack[T]) Len() int {
43: 	s.mu.Lock()
44: 	defer s.mu.Unlock()
45: 	return len(s.items)
46: }<|end|>

Returns list of errors.\"\"\"\n    errors: List[ValidationError] = []\n\n    # Type check\n    if \"type\" in schema:\n        expected = TYPE_MAP.get(schema[\"type\"])\n        if expected and not isinstance(data, expected):\n            errors.append(ValidationError(path, f\"expected type {schema['type']}\"))\n            return errors  # Skip further checks if type is wrong\n\n    # Required fields (for objects)\n    if \"required\" in schema and isinstance(data, dict):\n        for field in schema[\"required\"]:\n            if field not in data:\n                errors.append(ValidationError(f\"{path}.{field}\", \"is required\"))\n\n    # String constraints\n    if \"minLength\" in schema and isinstance(data, str):\n        if len(data) < schema[\"minLength\"]:\n            errors.append(ValidationError(\n                path, f\"must be at least {schema['minLength']} characters\"\n            ))\n\n    # Number constraints\n    if \"maximum\" in schema and isinstance(data, (int, float)):\n        if data > schema[\"maximum\"]:\n            errors.append(ValidationError(\n                path, f\"must be at most {schema['maximum']}\"\n            ))\n\n    # Recurse into properties\n    if \"properties\" in schema and isinstance(data, dict):\n        for prop, prop_schema in schema[\"properties\"].items():\n            if prop in data:\n                errors.extend(validate(prop_schema, data[prop], f\"{path}.{prop}\"))\n\n    return errors\n", "start_line": 1, "end_line": 17}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>analysis<|message|>Actually wait - the error message says 'temporary value' but self.input is owned by the struct. Let me look more carefully. The issue is that `self.input[start..self.pos]` borrows self.input, then on line 20, self.pos is mutated (self.pos += 1), which means there's a mutable borrow of self while the immutable borrow (token) is still alive. The borrow checker can't allow both. The fix: either return an owned String, or compute the slice indices and return the slice after the mutation. Let me restructure to do the pos increment before creating the borrow.<|end|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.replace_lines <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"file_path": "src/calc.rs", "start_line": 1, "end_line": 19, "content": "use std::collections::HashMap;\n\ntype OpFn = Box<dyn Fn(f64, f64) -> Result<f64, String>>;\n\npub struct Calculator {\n    ops: HashMap<String, OpFn>,\n}\n\nimpl Calculator {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        let mut calc = Calculator { ops: HashMap::new() };\n        calc.register(\"+\", |a, b| Ok(a + b));\n        calc.register(\"-\", |a, b| Ok(a - b));\n        calc.register(\"*\", |a, b| Ok(a * b));\n        calc.register(\"/\", |a, b| {\n            if b == 0.0 { return Err(\"division by zero\".to_string()); }\n            Ok(a / b)\n        });\n        calc.register(\"**\", |a, b| Ok(a.powf(b)));\n        calc.register(\"%\", |a, b| {\n            if b == 0.0 { return Err(\"modulo by zero\".to_string()); }\n            Ok(a % b)\n        });\n        calc.register(\"min\", |a, b| Ok(a.min(b)));\n        calc.register(\"max\", |a, b| Ok(a.max(b)));\n        calc\n    }\n\n    pub fn register(&mut self, op: &str, f: impl Fn(f64, f64) -> Result<f64, String> + 'static) {\n        self.ops.insert(op.to_string(), Box::new(f));\n    }\n\n    pub fn evaluate(&self, op: &str, a: f64, b: f64) -> Result<f64, String> {\n        match self.ops.get(op) {\n            Some(f) => f(a, b),\n            None => Err(format!(\"unknown operator: {}\", op)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Backward-compatible free function\npub fn evaluate(op: &str, a: f64, b: f64) -> Result<f64, String> {\n    Calculator::new().evaluate(op, a, b)\n}"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.replace_lines <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"file_path": "src/template_engine.py", "start_line": 1, "end_line": 12, "content": "import re\nfrom typing import Dict, Optional\n\nclass TemplateEngine:\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.templates: Dict[str, str] = {}\n\n    def register(self, name: str, content: str):\n        self.templates[name] = content\n\n    def render(self, name: str, context: Dict[str, str] = None) -> str:\n        context = context or {}\n        content = self.templates.get(name, '')\n\n        # Check for extends directive\n        extends_match = re.match(r\"\\{%\\s*extends\\s+'(\\w+)'\\s*%\\}\", content)\n        if extends_match:\n            parent_name = extends_match.group(1)\n            parent_content = self.templates.get(parent_name, '')\n            child_content = content[extends_match.end():]\n\n            # Extract child blocks\n            child_blocks = dict(re.findall(\n                r\"\\{%\\s*block\\s+(\\w+)\\s*%\\}(.*?)\\{%\\s*endblock\\s*%\\}\",\n                child_content, re.DOTALL))\n\n            # Replace parent blocks with child overrides\n            def replace_block(m):\n                block_name = m.group(1)\n                default = m.group(2)\n                return child_blocks.get(block_name, default)\n\n            result = re.sub(\n                r\"\\{%\\s*block\\s+(\\w+)\\s*%\\}(.*?)\\{%\\s*endblock\\s*%\\}\",\n                replace_block, parent_content, flags=re.DOTALL)\n        else:\n            # Remove block tags for standalone templates\n            result = re.sub(\n                r\"\\{%\\s*block\\s+\\w+\\s*%\\}(.*?)\\{%\\s*endblock\\s*%\\}\",\n                r'\\1', content, flags=re.DOTALL)\n\n        # Variable substitution\n        for key, value in context.items():\n            result = result.replace('{{ ' + key + ' }}', value)\n        return result.strip()\n"}<|call|>

store.append('account-1', { type: 'Created', data: { name: 'Alice' } });\n    store.append('account-1', { type: 'Deposited', data: { amount: 100 } });\n    const events = store.getEvents('account-1');\n    expect(events).toHaveLength(2);\n    expect(events[0].type).toBe('Created');\n    expect(events[1].type).toBe('Deposited');\n  });\n\n  test('events have auto-incremented version', () => {\n    store.append('s1', { type: 'A', data: {} });\n    store.append('s1', { type: 'B', data: {} });\n    const events = store.getEvents('s1');\n    expect(events[0].version).toBe(1);\n    expect(events[1].version).toBe(2);\n  });\n\n  test('replays events to rebuild state', () => {\n    store.append('cart-1', { type: 'ItemAdded', data: { item: 'apple', qty: 2 } });\n    store.append('cart-1', { type: 'ItemAdded', data: { item: 'banana', qty: 1 } });\n    store.append('cart-1', { type: 'ItemRemoved', data: { item: 'apple' } });\n\n    const state = store.replay<Record<string, number>>('cart-1', {}, (state, event) => {\n      if (event.type === 'ItemAdded') {\n        return { ...state, [event.data.item]: (state[event.data.item] || 0) + event.data.qty };\n      }\n      if (event.type === 'ItemRemoved') {\n        const { [event.data.item]: _, ...rest } = state;\n        return rest;\n      }\n      return state;\n    });\n\n    expect(state).toEqual({ banana: 1 });\n  });\n\n  test('snapshot skips replaying old events', () => {\n    store.append('acc-1', { type: 'Deposit', data: { amount: 100 } });\n    store.append('acc-1', { type: 'Deposit', data: { amount: 50 } });\n    store.saveSnapshot('acc-1', 2, { balance: 150 });\n    store.append('acc-1', { type: 'Deposit', data: { amount: 25 } });\n\n    let replayCount = 0;\n    const state = store.replay<{ balance: number }>('acc-1', { balance: 0 }, (state, event) => {\n      replayCount++;\n      return { balance: state.balance + event.data.amount };\n    });\n\n    expect(state.balance).toBe(175);  // 150 from snapshot + 25 from new event\n    expect(replayCount).toBe(1);  // only replayed 1 event after snapshot\n  });\n\n  test('streams are independent', () => {\n    store.append('s1', { type: 'X', data: {} });\n    store.append('s2', { type: 'Y', data: {} });\n    expect(store.getEvents('s1')).toHaveLength(1);\n    expect(store.getEvents('s2')).toHaveLength(1);\n    expect(store.getEvents('s3')).toHaveLength(0);\n  });\n});\n"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "src/eventStore.ts", "content": "export interface Event {\n  type: string;\n  data: any;\n  version?: number;\n  timestamp?: Date;\n}\n\ninterface StoredEvent extends Event {\n  version: number;\n  timestamp: Date;\n}\n\ninterface Snapshot {\n  version: number;\n  state: any;\n}\n\nexport class EventStore {\n  private streams: Map<string, StoredEvent[]> = new Map();\n  private snapshots: Map<string, Snapshot> = new Map();\n\n  append(streamId: string, event: Event): StoredEvent {\n    if (!this.streams.has(streamId)) {\n      this.streams.set(streamId, []);\n    }\n    const events = this.streams.get(streamId)!;\n    const stored: StoredEvent = {\n      ...event,\n      version: events.length + 1,\n      timestamp: new Date(),\n    };\n    events.push(stored);\n    return stored;\n  }\n\n  getEvents(streamId: string, afterVersion: number = 0): StoredEvent[] {\n    const events = this.streams.get(streamId) || [];\n    return events.filter(e => e.version > afterVersion);\n  }\n\n  replay<T>(streamId: string, initialState: T, reducer: (state: T, event: StoredEvent) => T): T {\n    const snapshot = this.snapshots.get(streamId);\n    let state = initialState;\n    let fromVersion = 0;\n\n    if (snapshot) {\n      state = snapshot.state as T;\n      fromVersion = snapshot.version;\n    }\n\n    const events = this.getEvents(streamId, fromVersion);\n    for (const event of events) {\n      state = reducer(state, event);\n    }\n    return state;\n  }\n\n  saveSnapshot(streamId: string, version: number, state: any): void {\n    this.snapshots.set(streamId, { version, state });\n  }\n}\n"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "src/services/webhookHandler.ts", "content": "import Stripe from 'stripe';\nimport { OrderService } from './orderService';\nimport { config } from '../config';\n\nexport class WebhookHandler {\n  private orderService: OrderService;\n\n  constructor(orderService: OrderService) {\n    this.orderService = orderService;\n  }\n\n  verifySignature(payload: string | Buffer, signature: string): Stripe.Event {\n    const stripe = new Stripe(config.stripeSecretKey);\n    return stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(\n      payload,\n      signature,\n      config.stripeWebhookSecret\n    );\n  }\n\n  handleEvent(event: Stripe.Event): { status: string; orderId?: string } {\n    switch (event.type) {\n      case 'payment_intent.succeeded': {\n        const intent = event.data.object as Stripe.PaymentIntent;\n        const order = this.orderService.findByPaymentIntent(intent.id);\n        if (order) {\n          this.orderService.updateStatus(order.id, 'paid');\n          return { status: 'updated', orderId: order.id };\n        }\n        return { status: 'order_not_found' };\n      }\n      case 'payment_intent.payment_failed': {\n        const intent = event.data.object as Stripe.PaymentIntent;\n        const order = this.orderService.findByPaymentIntent(intent.id);\n        if (order) {\n          this.orderService.updateStatus(order.id, 'failed');\n          return { status: 'updated', orderId: order.id };\n        }\n        return { status: 'order_not_found' };\n      }\n      default:\n        return { status: 'unhandled' };\n    }\n  }\n}\n"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.replace_lines <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"file_path": "src/task_queue.py", "start_line": 1, "end_line": 20, "content": "import heapq\nfrom typing import Callable, Optional\nfrom enum import IntEnum\n\nclass Priority(IntEnum):\n    HIGH = 0\n    NORMAL = 1\n    LOW = 2\n\nclass Task:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, fn: Callable, priority: Priority = Priority.NORMAL):\n        self.name = name\n        self.fn = fn\n        self.priority = priority\n        self._order = 0  # insertion order for stable sort\n\n    def __lt__(self, other):\n        if self.priority != other.priority:\n            return self.priority < other.priority\n        return self._order < other._order\n\nclass TaskQueue:\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._heap = []\n        self._counter = 0\n\n    def push(self, task: Task):\n        task._order = self._counter\n        self._counter += 1\n        heapq.heappush(self._heap, task)\n\n    def pop(self) -> Optional[Task]:\n        return heapq.heappop(self._heap) if self._heap else None\n\n    def size(self) -> int:\n        return len(self._heap)"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "src/retrylib/retry.py", "content": "\"\"\"Retry decorator with configurable backoff.\"\"\"\nimport functools\nimport time\nimport logging\nfrom typing import Callable, Tuple, Type\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\ndef retry(\n    max_attempts: int = 3,\n    delay: float = 1.0,\n    backoff_factor: float = 2.0,\n    retry_on: Tuple[Type[Exception], ...] = (Exception,),\n) -> Callable:\n    \"\"\"Decorator that retries a function on failure.\"\"\"\n    def decorator(func: Callable) -> Callable:\n        @functools.wraps(func)\n        def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):\n            last_exc = None\n            current_delay = delay\n            for attempt in range(1, max_attempts + 1):\n                try:\n                    return func(*args, **kwargs)\n                except retry_on as exc:\n                    last_exc = exc\n                    if attempt < max_attempts:\n                        logger.warning(\n                            \"Attempt %d/%d failed for %s: %s. Retrying in %.1fs\",\n                            attempt, max_attempts, func.__name__, exc, current_delay,\n                        )\n                        time.sleep(current_delay)\n                        current_delay *= backoff_factor\n            raise last_exc\n        return wrapper\n    return decorator\n"}<|call|>

2: import re

4: 
5: 
6: class ParseError(Exception):

11:     pass
12: 
13: 
14: def evaluate(expr: str) -> float:
15:     """Evaluate arithmetic expression with +, -, *, /, and parentheses."""
16:     tokens = tokenize(expr)
17:     result, pos = parse_expression(tokens, 0)
18:     if pos != len(tokens):
19:         raise ParseError(f"Unexpected token at position {pos}")
20:     return result

22: 
23: def tokenize(expr: str) -> List[str]:
24:     return re.findall(r'\d+\.?\d*|[+\-*/()]', expr)
25: 
26: 
27: def parse_expression(tokens, pos):
28:     left, pos = parse_term(tokens, pos)
29:     while pos < len(tokens) and tokens[pos] in ('+', '-'):
30:         op = tokens[pos]
31:         right, pos = parse_term(tokens, pos + 1)
32:         left = left + right if op == '+' else left - right
33:     return left, pos
34: 
35: 
36: def parse_term(tokens, pos):
37:     left, pos = parse_factor(tokens, pos)
38:     while pos < len(tokens) and tokens[pos] in ('*', '/'):
39:         op = tokens[pos]
40:         right, pos = parse_factor(tokens, pos + 1)
41:         if op == '/':
42:             if right == 0:
43:                 raise DivisionByZeroError("Division by zero")
44:             left = left / right
45:         else:
46:             left = left * right
47:     return left, pos
48: 
49: 
50: def parse_factor(tokens, pos):
51:     if pos >= len(tokens):
52:         raise ParseError("Unexpected end of expression")
53:     if tokens[pos] == '(':
54:         result, pos = parse_expression(tokens, pos + 1)
55:         if pos >= len(tokens) or tokens[pos] != ')':
56:             raise ParseError("Missing closing parenthesis")
57:         return result, pos + 1
58:     try:
59:         return float(tokens[pos]), pos + 1

The job queue has two issues: 1) Jobs sometimes run twice (duplicate execution), 2) Failed jobs are not being retried despite the retry configuration. Fix both.
</task><|end|>

<|start|>functions.read_file to=assistant<|channel|>commentary<|message|>1: package queue
2: 
3: import "sync"
4: 
5: type Job struct {
6: 	ID       string
7: 	Payload  []byte
8: 	Retries  int
9: 	MaxRetry int
10: 	Status   string // pending, running, done, failed
11: }
12: 
13: type Queue struct {
14: 	mu   sync.Mutex
15: 	jobs []*Job
16: }
17: 
18: func New() *Queue {
19: 	return &Queue{}
20: }
21: 
22: func (q *Queue) Push(job *Job) {
23: 	q.mu.Lock()
24: 	defer q.mu.Unlock()
25: 	job.Status = "pending"
26: 	q.jobs = append(q.jobs, job)
27: }
28: 
29: func (q *Queue) Next() *Job {
30: 	q.mu.Lock()
31: 	defer q.mu.Unlock()
32: 	for _, job := range q.jobs {
33: 		if job.Status == "pending" {
34: 			return job
35: 		}
36: 	}
37: 	return nil
38: }
39: 
40: func (q *Queue) Complete(id string) {
41: 	q.mu.Lock()
42: 	defer q.mu.Unlock()
43: 	for _, job := range q.jobs {
44: 		if job.ID == id {
45: 			job.Status = "done"
46: 		}
47: 	}
48: }
49: 
50: func (q *Queue) Fail(id string) {
51: 	q.mu.Lock()
52: 	defer q.mu.Unlock()
53: 	for _, job := range q.jobs {
54: 		if job.ID == id {
55: 			job.Status = "failed"
56: 			job.Retries++
57: 		}
58: 	}
59: }<|end|>
<|start|>assistant<|channel|>analysis<|message|>Found both bugs:

1. Duplicate execution (line 32-35): Next() finds a pending job and returns it, but DOESN'T set its status to 'running'. So the next worker calling Next() will also find the same 'pending' job and pick it up → duplicate execution.

2. Failed jobs not retried (line 50-58): Fail() sets status to 'failed' and increments Retries, but never checks if Retries < MaxRetry to re-queue. If the job has retries remaining, it should be set back to 'pending' instead of 'failed'.<|end|>

Duplicate execution: Next() (line 32-35) returns pending jobs without marking them as 'running'. Multiple workers call Next() and get the same job.\n2. No retry: Fail() (line 50-58) always sets status='failed'. Never checks Retries < MaxRetry to re-queue as 'pending'.", "status": "continue", "next_steps": ["Fix Next() to mark jobs as running", "Fix Fail() to retry when under max"]}<|call|>

Python verification:
import hmac
import hashlib

def verify_signature(payload_body, secret_token, signature_header):
    hash_object = hmac.new(secret_token.encode('utf-8'), msg=payload_body, digestmod=hashlib.sha256)
    expected = "sha256=" + hash_object.hexdigest()
    return hmac.compare_digest(expected, signature_header)

Always use constant-time comparison (hmac.compare_digest) to prevent timing attacks.<|end|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "src/export.rs", "content": "use anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse csv::Writer;\nuse crate::db;\n\npub fn run(output: &str, table: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let conn = db::connect()?;\n    let mut stmt = conn.prepare(&format!(\"SELECT * FROM {}\", table))\n        .with_context(|| format!(\"Table '{}' not found\", table))?;\n    let col_count = stmt.column_count();\n    let columns: Vec<String> = (0..col_count)\n        .map(|i| stmt.column_name(i).unwrap().to_string())\n        .collect();\n\n    let mut wtr = Writer::from_path(output)\n        .with_context(|| format!(\"Cannot write to {}\", output))?;\n    wtr.write_record(&columns)?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n    while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        let values: Vec<String> = (0..col_count)\n            .map(|i| row.get::<_, String>(i).unwrap_or_default())\n            .collect();\n        wtr.write_record(&values)?;\n    }\n    wtr.flush()?;\n    println!(\"Exported to {}\", output);\n    Ok(())\n}\n"}<|call|>

13: def merge(left: List[int], right: List[int]) -> List[int]:
14:     result = []

18:             result.append(left[i])
19:             i += 1
20:         else:
21:             result.append(right[j])

<|start|>functions.help to=assistant<|channel|>commentary<|message|>EDIT TOOLS — Modifying existing files

TWO APPROACHES:

1. SYMBOLIC (preferred for methods/functions):
   edit_symbol(symbol, new_code)  — Replace a method/function by name
   add_symbol(code, file_path)    — Add a method to a file or class
   remove_symbol(symbol)          — Remove a method by name

2. LINE-BASED (for anything else):
   replace_lines(file_path, content, start_line, end_line)
   — Deterministic: specify exact line range, no text matching needed
   — Use read_file first to see line numbers

3. INSERT (add new content without removing):
   add_content_after_line(file_path, line_number, content)
   add_content_before_line(file_path, line_number, content)

4. REFACTORING (project-wide operations):
   rename_symbol(symbol, new_name)  — Rename everywhere: definition + all references + imports
   move_symbol(symbol, target_file) — Move to another file/class, update imports

WORKFLOW:
  1. read_file("src/foo.py")           → see the code with line numbers
  2. edit_symbol("Foo.bar", new_code)  → replace method by name
  OR
  2. replace_lines("src/foo.py", new_code, 10, 25)  → replace lines 10-25<|end|>

import "time"

// Note represents a single note with title, content, and tags.
type Note struct {
	ID        string    `json:"id"`
	Title     string    `json:"title"`
	Content   string    `json:"content"`
	Tags      []string  `json:"tags"`
	CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
	UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
}

// CreateNoteRequest is the JSON body for creating/updating a note.
type CreateNoteRequest struct {
	Title   string   `json:"title"`
	Content string   `json:"content"`
	Tags    []string `json:"tags"`
}

// ErrorResponse is the JSON body for error responses.
type ErrorResponse struct {
	Error string `json:"error"`
}
"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "store.go", "content": "package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"sync"
	"time"
)

// NoteStore provides thread-safe in-memory storage for notes.
type NoteStore struct {
	mu      sync.RWMutex
	notes   map[string]*Note
	counter int
}

// NewNoteStore creates an empty store.
func NewNoteStore() *NoteStore {
	return &NoteStore{
		notes: make(map[string]*Note),
	}
}

// Create adds a new note and returns it.
func (s *NoteStore) Create(req CreateNoteRequest) *Note {
	s.mu.Lock()
	defer s.mu.Unlock()

	s.counter++
	note := &Note{
		ID:        fmt.Sprintf("%d", s.counter),
		Title:     req.Title,
		Content:   req.Content,
		Tags:      req.Tags,
		CreatedAt: time.Now(),
		UpdatedAt: time.Now(),
	}
	if note.Tags == nil {
		note.Tags = []string{}
	}
	s.notes[note.ID] = note
	return note
}

// GetAll returns all notes.
func (s *NoteStore) GetAll() []*Note {
	s.mu.RLock()
	defer s.mu.RUnlock()

	notes := make([]*Note, 0, len(s.notes))
	for _, n := range s.notes {
		notes = append(notes, n)
	}
	return notes
}

// GetByID returns a note by ID, or nil if not found.
func (s *NoteStore) GetByID(id string) *Note {
	s.mu.RLock()
	defer s.mu.RUnlock()
	return s.notes[id]
}

// Update modifies an existing note. Returns the updated note or nil if not found.
func (s *NoteStore) Update(id string, req CreateNoteRequest) *Note {
	s.mu.Lock()
	defer s.mu.Unlock()

	note, exists := s.notes[id]
	if !exists {
		return nil
	}
	note.Title = req.Title
	note.Content = req.Content
	note.Tags = req.Tags
	if note.Tags == nil {
		note.Tags = []string{}
	}
	note.UpdatedAt = time.Now()
	return note
}

// Delete removes a note by ID. Returns true if deleted, false if not found.
func (s *NoteStore) Delete(id string) bool {
	s.mu.Lock()
	defer s.mu.Unlock()

	if _, exists := s.notes[id]; !exists {
		return false
	}
	delete(s.notes, id)
	return true
}

// FilterByTag returns notes that contain the given tag.
func (s *NoteStore) FilterByTag(tag string) []*Note {
	s.mu.RLock()
	defer s.mu.RUnlock()

	var result []*Note
	for _, n := range s.notes {
		for _, t := range n.Tags {
			if t == tag {
				result = append(result, n)
				break
			}
		}
	}
	return result
}
"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "helpers.go", "content": "package main

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"net/http"
)

// respondJSON writes a JSON response with the given status code.
func respondJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, data interface{}) {
	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	w.WriteHeader(status)
	if data != nil {
		json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(data)
	}
}

// respondError writes a JSON error response.
func respondError(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, message string) {
	respondJSON(w, status, ErrorResponse{Error: message})
}

// decodeJSON reads JSON from the request body into dst.
func decodeJSON(r *http.Request, dst interface{}) error {
	return json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(dst)
}
"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "main.go", "content": "package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"net/http"
	"strings"
)

// Server holds the store and implements HTTP handlers.
type Server struct {
	store *NoteStore
}

// NewServer creates a server with a fresh store.
func NewServer() *Server {
	return &Server{store: NewNoteStore()}
}

// handleCreateNote handles POST /notes
func (s *Server) handleCreateNote(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	var req CreateNoteRequest
	if err := decodeJSON(r, &req); err != nil {
		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid JSON body")
		return
	}
	if req.Title == "" {
		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "title is required")
		return
	}
	note := s.store.Create(req)
	respondJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, note)
}

// handleGetNotes handles GET /notes with optional ?tag= filter
func (s *Server) handleGetNotes(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	tag := r.URL.Query().Get("tags")
	var notes []*Note
	if tag != "" {
		notes = s.store.FilterByTag(tag)
	} else {
		notes = s.store.GetAll()
	}
	if notes == nil {
		notes = []*Note{}
	}
	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, notes)
}

// handleGetNote handles GET /notes/{id}
func (s *Server) handleGetNote(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	id := r.PathValue("id")
	note := s.store.GetByID(id)
	if note == nil {
		respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, fmt.Sprintf("note %s not found", id))
		return
	}
	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, note)
}

// handleUpdateNote handles PUT /notes/{id}
func (s *Server) handleUpdateNote(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	id := r.PathValue("id")
	var req CreateNoteRequest
	if err := decodeJSON(r, &req); err != nil {
		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid JSON body")
		return
	}
	if req.Title == "" {
		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "title is required")
		return
	}
	note := s.store.Update(id, req)
	if note == nil {
		respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, fmt.Sprintf("note %s not found", id))
		return
	}
	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, note)
}

// handleDeleteNote handles DELETE /notes/{id}
func (s *Server) handleDeleteNote(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	id := r.PathValue("id")
	if !s.store.Delete(id) {
		respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, fmt.Sprintf("note %s not found", id))
		return
	}
	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
}

// SetupRoutes configures the HTTP routes on the given mux.
func (s *Server) SetupRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux) {
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /notes", s.handleCreateNote)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /notes", s.handleGetNotes)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /notes/{id}", s.handleGetNote)
	mux.HandleFunc("PUT /notes/{id}", s.handleUpdateNote)
	mux.HandleFunc("DELETE /notes/{id}", s.handleDeleteNote)
}

func main() {
	server := NewServer()
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	server.SetupRoutes(mux)

	addr := ":8080"
	log.Printf("Notes API server starting on %s", addr)
	log.Fatal(http.ListenAndServe(addr, mux))
}
"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "handlers_test.go", "content": "package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"encoding/json"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"testing"
)

// setupTestServer creates a fresh server with routes for testing.
func setupTestServer() *httptest.Server {
	srv := NewServer()
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	srv.SetupRoutes(mux)
	return httptest.NewServer(mux)
}

// createTestNote is a helper to POST a note and return the response.
func createTestNote(t *testing.T, ts *httptest.Server, title, content string, tags []string) *Note {
	t.Helper()
	body := CreateNoteRequest{Title: title, Content: content, Tags: tags}
	buf, _ := json.Marshal(body)
	resp, err := http.Post(ts.URL+"/notes", "application/json", bytes.NewReader(buf))
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("POST /notes failed: %v", err)
	}
	defer resp.Body.Close()
	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusCreated {
		t.Fatalf("expected 201, got %d", resp.StatusCode)
	}
	var note Note
	json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&note)
	return &note
}

func TestCreateNote(t *testing.T) {
	ts := setupTestServer()
	defer ts.Close()

	note := createTestNote(t, ts, "Test Note", "Some content", []string{"work", "urgent"})
	if note.ID == "" {
		t.Error("expected note to have an ID")
	}
	if note.Title != "Test Note" {
		t.Errorf("expected title %q, got %q", "Test Note", note.Title)
	}
	if len(note.Tags) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("expected 2 tags, got %d", len(note.Tags))
	}
}

func TestCreateNoteValidation(t *testing.T) {
	ts := setupTestServer()
	defer ts.Close()

	body := CreateNoteRequest{Content: "no title"}
	buf, _ := json.Marshal(body)
	resp, err := http.Post(ts.URL+"/notes", "application/json", bytes.NewReader(buf))
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("POST /notes failed: %v", err)
	}
	defer resp.Body.Close()
	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusBadRequest {
		t.Errorf("expected 400 for missing title, got %d", resp.StatusCode)
	}
}

func TestCreateNoteInvalidJSON(t *testing.T) {
	ts := setupTestServer()
	defer ts.Close()

	resp, err := http.Post(ts.URL+"/notes", "application/json", bytes.NewReader([]byte("not json")))
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("POST /notes failed: %v", err)
	}
	defer resp.Body.Close()
	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusBadRequest {
		t.Errorf("expected 400 for invalid JSON, got %d", resp.StatusCode)
	}
}

func TestGetNotes(t *testing.T) {
	ts := setupTestServer()
	defer ts.Close()

	resp, _ := http.Get(ts.URL + "/notes")
	var notes []Note
	json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&notes)
	resp.Body.Close()
	if len(notes) != 0 {
		t.Errorf("expected 0 notes, got %d", len(notes))
	}

	createTestNote(t, ts, "Note 1", "Content 1", nil)
	createTestNote(t, ts, "Note 2", "Content 2", nil)

	resp, _ = http.Get(ts.URL + "/notes")
	json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&notes)
	resp.Body.Close()
	if len(notes) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("expected 2 notes, got %d", len(notes))
	}
}

func TestGetNoteByID(t *testing.T) {
	ts := setupTestServer()
	defer ts.Close()

	note := createTestNote(t, ts, "Find Me", "Content", []string{"search"})

	resp, _ := http.Get(ts.URL + "/notes/" + note.ID)
	var found Note
	json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&found)
	resp.Body.Close()

	if found.Title != "Find Me" {
		t.Errorf("expected title %q, got %q", "Find Me", found.Title)
	}
}

func TestGetNoteNotFound(t *testing.T) {
	ts := setupTestServer()
	defer ts.Close()

	resp, _ := http.Get(ts.URL + "/notes/999")
	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusNotFound {
		t.Errorf("expected 404, got %d", resp.StatusCode)
	}
	resp.Body.Close()
}

func TestUpdateNote(t *testing.T) {
	ts := setupTestServer()
	defer ts.Close()

	note := createTestNote(t, ts, "Original", "Content", []string{"v1"})

	updateReq := CreateNoteRequest{Title: "Updated", Content: "New content", Tags: []string{"v2"}}
	buf, _ := json.Marshal(updateReq)
	req, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, ts.URL+"/notes/"+note.ID, bytes.NewReader(buf))
	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("PUT failed: %v", err)
	}
	defer resp.Body.Close()

	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
		t.Errorf("expected 200, got %d", resp.StatusCode)
	}

	var updated Note
	json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&updated)
	if updated.Title != "Updated" {
		t.Errorf("expected title %q, got %q", "Updated", updated.Title)
	}
	if updated.Tags[0] != "v2" {
		t.Errorf("expected tag %q, got %q", "v2", updated.Tags[0])
	}
}

func TestUpdateNoteNotFound(t *testing.T) {
	ts := setupTestServer()
	defer ts.Close()

	updateReq := CreateNoteRequest{Title: "Ghost", Content: "Content"}
	buf, _ := json.Marshal(updateReq)
	req, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, ts.URL+"/notes/999", bytes.NewReader(buf))
	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	resp, _ := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusNotFound {
		t.Errorf("expected 404, got %d", resp.StatusCode)
	}
	resp.Body.Close()
}

func TestDeleteNote(t *testing.T) {
	ts := setupTestServer()
	defer ts.Close()

	note := createTestNote(t, ts, "Delete Me", "Content", nil)

	req, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodDelete, ts.URL+"/notes/"+note.ID, nil)
	resp, _ := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusNoContent {
		t.Errorf("expected 204, got %d", resp.StatusCode)
	}
	resp.Body.Close()

	resp2, _ := http.Get(ts.URL + "/notes/" + note.ID)
	if resp2.StatusCode != http.StatusNotFound {
		t.Errorf("expected 404 after delete, got %d", resp2.StatusCode)
	}
	resp2.Body.Close()
}

func TestDeleteNoteNotFound(t *testing.T) {
	ts := setupTestServer()
	defer ts.Close()

	req, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodDelete, ts.URL+"/notes/999", nil)
	resp, _ := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusNotFound {
		t.Errorf("expected 404, got %d", resp.StatusCode)
	}
	resp.Body.Close()
}

func TestFilterByTag(t *testing.T) {
	ts := setupTestServer()
	defer ts.Close()

	createTestNote(t, ts, "Work Note", "Content", []string{"work", "important"})
	createTestNote(t, ts, "Personal Note", "Content", []string{"personal"})
	createTestNote(t, ts, "Work Meeting", "Content", []string{"work", "meeting"})

	resp, _ := http.Get(ts.URL + "/notes?tag=work")
	var notes []Note
	json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&notes)
	resp.Body.Close()

	if len(notes) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("expected 2 work notes, got %d", len(notes))
	}
}

func TestFilterByTagNoResults(t *testing.T) {
	ts := setupTestServer()
	defer ts.Close()

	createTestNote(t, ts, "Note", "Content", []string{"alpha"})

	resp, _ := http.Get(ts.URL + "/notes?tag=nonexistent")
	var notes []Note
	json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&notes)
	resp.Body.Close()

	if len(notes) != 0 {
		t.Errorf("expected 0 notes for nonexistent tag, got %d", len(notes))
	}
}

func TestNoteTagsDefaultToEmptySlice(t *testing.T) {
	ts := setupTestServer()
	defer ts.Close()

	note := createTestNote(t, ts, "No Tags", "Content", nil)
	if note.Tags == nil {
		t.Error("expected tags to be empty slice, not nil")
	}
	if len(note.Tags) != 0 {
		t.Errorf("expected 0 tags, got %d", len(note.Tags))
	}
}

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "store_test.go", "content": "package main

import (
	"testing"
)

func TestStoreFilterByTag(t *testing.T) {
	s := NewNoteStore()
	s.Create(CreateNoteRequest{Title: "A", Content: "C", Tags: []string{"work", "important"}})
	s.Create(CreateNoteRequest{Title: "B", Content: "C", Tags: []string{"personal"}})
	s.Create(CreateNoteRequest{Title: "C", Content: "C", Tags: []string{"work", "meeting"}})

	result := s.FilterByTag("work")
	if len(result) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("expected 2 work notes from store, got %d", len(result))
		for _, n := range s.GetAll() {
			t.Logf("  note %s: tags=%v", n.Title, n.Tags)
		}
	}
}

func TestStoreCreateRetainsTags(t *testing.T) {
	s := NewNoteStore()
	note := s.Create(CreateNoteRequest{Title: "Test", Content: "C", Tags: []string{"a", "b"}})
	if len(note.Tags) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("expected 2 tags, got %d: %v", len(note.Tags), note.Tags)
	}

	stored := s.GetByID(note.ID)
	if len(stored.Tags) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("stored note expected 2 tags, got %d: %v", len(stored.Tags), stored.Tags)
	}
}

func TestStoreDeleteRemovesNote(t *testing.T) {
	s := NewNoteStore()
	note := s.Create(CreateNoteRequest{Title: "Del", Content: "C"})
	if !s.Delete(note.ID) {
		t.Error("Delete returned false for existing note")
	}
	if s.GetByID(note.ID) != nil {
		t.Error("GetByID returned non-nil after delete")
	}
	if s.Delete(note.ID) {
		t.Error("Delete returned true for already-deleted note")
	}
}
"}<|call|>

**Configuration:**
- `go.mod` — Go 1.22 module, no external dependencies

**Source:**
- `models.go` — Note, CreateNoteRequest, ErrorResponse types with JSON tags
- `store.go` — NoteStore with sync.RWMutex for thread-safe CRUD (Create, GetAll, GetByID, Update, Delete, FilterByTag). Tags default to empty slice on create/update.
- `helpers.go` — JSON response utilities: respondJSON, respondError, decodeJSON
- `main.go` — Server with 5 HTTP handlers using Go 1.22 method-based ServeMux routing: POST /notes (create with title validation), GET /notes (list with ?tag= filter), GET /notes/{id}, PUT /notes/{id}, DELETE /notes/{id}. Proper error responses (400, 404, 204).

**Tests:**
- `handlers_test.go` — 13 httptest-based integration tests covering all endpoints, validation, error cases, tag filtering, and edge cases.
- `store_test.go` — 3 unit tests: FilterByTag, tag retention after create, delete behavior.

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "linter/watcher.py", "content": "import time\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import List, Callable, Dict\n\n\nclass FileWatcher:\n    def __init__(self, files: List[Path], callback: Callable[[List[Path]], None], interval: float = 1.0):\n        self.files = files\n        self.callback = callback\n        self.interval = interval\n        self._mtimes: Dict[Path, float] = {}\n        self._running = False\n\n    def _get_mtimes(self) -> Dict[Path, float]:\n        mtimes = {}\n        for f in self.files:\n            if f.exists():\n                mtimes[f] = f.stat().st_mtime\n        return mtimes\n\n    def _check_changes(self) -> List[Path]:\n        current = self._get_mtimes()\n        changed = []\n        for f, mtime in current.items():\n            if f not in self._mtimes or self._mtimes[f] != mtime:\n                changed.append(f)\n        self._mtimes = current\n        return changed\n\n    def start(self):\n        self._mtimes = self._get_mtimes()\n        self._running = True\n        # Initial run\n        self.callback(self.files)\n        while self._running:\n            time.sleep(self.interval)\n            changed = self._check_changes()\n            if changed:\n                print(f'\\n--- Files changed: {\", \".join(str(f) for f in changed)} ---')\n                self.callback(changed)\n\n    def stop(self):\n        self._running = False\n"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.replace_lines <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"file_path": "linter/cli.py", "start_line": 1, "end_line": 25, "content": "import argparse\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom .checker import Checker\n\n\ndef run_lint(checker: Checker, files: list) -> int:\n    total_issues = 0\n    for filepath in files:\n        issues = checker.check(filepath)\n        for issue in issues:\n            print(f'{filepath}:{issue.line}: {issue.message}')\n        total_issues += len(issues)\n    if total_issues > 0:\n        print(f'\\nFound {total_issues} issues')\n    else:\n        print('No issues found')\n    return total_issues\n\n\ndef main():\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='File linter')\n    parser.add_argument('files', nargs='+', type=Path)\n    parser.add_argument('--fix', action='store_true', help='Auto-fix issues')\n    parser.add_argument('--watch', action='store_true', help='Watch files for changes and re-lint')\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    checker = Checker()\n\n    if args.watch:\n        from .watcher import FileWatcher\n        watcher = FileWatcher(\n            files=args.files,\n            callback=lambda changed: run_lint(checker, changed),\n            interval=1.0\n        )\n        try:\n            print(f'Watching {len(args.files)} files for changes (Ctrl+C to stop)...')\n            watcher.start()\n        except KeyboardInterrupt:\n            watcher.stop()\n            print('\\nStopped watching.')\n    else:\n        total = run_lint(checker, args.files)\n        sys.exit(1 if total > 0 else 0)"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>analysis<|message|>The issue is the guard on line 7: `if (this.syncing) return`. When sync is called while a previous sync is still running (from setInterval or user-triggered), the new call is silently dropped. This means if changes happen during a sync, the next sync call is skipped, and those changes aren't picked up until the NEXT interval. The fix should either queue the sync request or track a 'dirty' flag that triggers another sync when the current one completes.<|end|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.send_message <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"message": "Found the issue in `SyncManager.sync()`. The `if (this.syncing) return` guard on line 7 silently drops sync requests that arrive while a sync is in progress. If changes happen during a sync, the next scheduled sync call is discarded, and those changes don't get picked up until the interval AFTER that.\n\nI'll fix this by tracking a 'pending' flag: if a sync is requested during an active sync, it's queued and runs immediately after the current one finishes."}<|call|>

<|start|>functions.help to=assistant<|channel|>commentary<|message|>## Editing Tools

**replace_lines(path, start_line, end_line, new_content)** — Replace a range of lines in a file. Lines are 1-indexed. The range is inclusive: start_line and end_line are both replaced. Use read_file or partial_read first to get accurate line numbers.

**add_content_before_line(path, line_number, content)** — Insert new content before the specified line number.

**add_content_after_line(path, line_number, content)** — Insert new content after the specified line number.

**create_file(path, content)** — Create a new file with the given content. Fails if the file already exists.

**edit_symbol(symbol, new_code)** — Replace a function/method/class by its qualified name (e.g. `MyClass.my_method`). Uses the code index.

**add_symbol(code, file_path, position)** — Add a new function/method to a file or class. Position: "end", "beginning", or "after:SymbolName".

**remove_symbol(symbol)** — Remove a function/method by its qualified name.

Best practices:
- Always read the file first to get accurate line numbers
- Use edit_symbol for modifying existing functions/methods
- Use add_symbol for adding new methods to classes
- Use replace_lines for non-symbol edits (imports, config values, etc.)<|end|>
<|start|>assistant<|channel|>analysis<|message|>I need to build a bookmark manager REST API from scratch. Let me plan the project structure:

Project layout:
  app/
    __init__.py    — Flask app factory with create_app()
    db.py          — SQLite connection management, schema creation
    models.py      — Bookmark dataclass + CRUD functions (get_all, get_by_id, create, update, delete, filter_by_tag)
    routes.py      — Blueprint with REST endpoints
  tests/
    conftest.py    — pytest fixtures (test client, temp database)
    test_api.py    — endpoint tests for all CRUD operations
  run.py           — entry point
  requirements.txt — flask, pytest

Database schema:
  bookmarks table: id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, url TEXT NOT NULL, title TEXT NOT NULL, tags TEXT (comma-separated), created_at TIMESTAMP, updated_at TIMESTAMP

Endpoints:
  GET    /api/bookmarks          — list all (with optional ?tag= filter)
  GET    /api/bookmarks/<id>     — get one
  POST   /api/bookmarks          — create
  PUT    /api/bookmarks/<id>     — update
  DELETE /api/bookmarks/<id>     — delete

I will build this step by step: db layer first, then models, then routes, then run.py, then test manually with curl, then write the test suite.<|end|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "app/__init__.py", "content": "import os\nfrom flask import Flask\n\n\ndef create_app(test_config=None):\n    \"\"\"Application factory for the bookmark manager API.\"\"\"\n    app = Flask(__name__, instance_relative_config=True)\n\n    # Default configuration\n    app.config.from_mapping(\n        DATABASE=os.path.join(app.instance_path, \"bookmarks.db\"),\n        TESTING=False,\n    )\n\n    if test_config is not None:\n        app.config.from_mapping(test_config)\n\n    # Ensure the instance folder exists\n    os.makedirs(app.instance_path, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Initialize database\n    from . import db\n    db.init_app(app)\n\n    # Register routes\n    from . import routes\n    app.register_blueprint(routes.bp)\n\n    return app\n"}<|call|>

<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "app/db.py", "content": "import sqlite3\nfrom flask import current_app, g\n\n\ndef get_db():\n    \"\"\"Get a database connection for the current request.\n\n    The connection is stored on the Flask g object and reused\n    within the same request context.\n    \"\"\"\n    if \"db\" not in g:\n        g.db = sqlite3.connect(\n            current_app.config[\"DATABASE\"],\n            detect_types=sqlite3.PARSE_DECLTYPES,\n        )\n        g.db.row_factory = sqlite3.Row\n    return g.db\n\n\ndef close_db(e=None):\n    \"\"\"Close the database connection at the end of a request.\"\"\"\n    db = g.pop(\"db\", None)\n    if db is not None:\n        db.close()\n\n\ndef init_db():\n    \"\"\"Create the bookmarks table if it does not exist.\"\"\"\n    db = get_db()\n    db.execute(\n        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (\"\n        \"  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\"\n        \"  url TEXT NOT NULL,\"\n        \"  title TEXT NOT NULL,\"\n        \"  tags TEXT DEFAULT '',\"\n        \"  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\"\n        \"  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\"\n        \")\"\n    )\n    db.commit()\n\n\ndef init_app(app):\n    \"\"\"Register database functions with the Flask app.\"\"\"\n    app.teardown_appcontext(close_db)\n    with app.app_context():\n        init_db()\n"}<|call|>
<|start|>functions.create_file to=assistant<|channel|>commentary<|message|>File created: app/db.py<|end|>
<|start|>assistant<|channel|>analysis<|message|>Now I need to create the models layer. I will use a dataclass for Bookmark and write CRUD functions that use app/db.py. The functions will handle:
- get_all_bookmarks(tag=None) — returns all bookmarks, optionally filtered by tag
- get_bookmark(bookmark_id) — returns one bookmark or None
- create_bookmark(url, title, tags) — inserts and returns the new bookmark
- update_bookmark(bookmark_id, url, title, tags) — updates and returns the bookmark
- delete_bookmark(bookmark_id) — deletes, returns True/False

Tags will be stored as a comma-separated string in the database. The model layer will convert between the string representation and a list of tags.<|end|>
<|start|>functions.create_file to=assistant<|channel|>commentary<|message|>[Thought recorded]<|end|>
<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=functions.create_file <|constrain|>json<|message|>{"path": "app/models.py", "content": "from dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import List, Optional\n\nfrom .db import get_db\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass Bookmark:\n    \"\"\"Represents a saved bookmark.\"\"\"\n    id: int\n    url: str\n    title: str\n    tags: List[str]\n    created_at: str\n    updated_at: str\n\n    def to_dict(self):\n        return {\n            \"id\": self.id,\n            \"url\": self.url,\n            \"title\": self.title,\n            \"tags\": self.tags,\n            \"created_at\": self.created_at,\n            \"updated_at\": self.updated_at,\n        }\n\n\ndef _row_to_bookmark(row) -> Bookmark:\n    \"\"\"Convert a database row to a Bookmark instance.\"\"\"\n    tags_str = row[\"tags\"] or \"\"\n    tags = [t.strip() for t in tags_str.split(\",\") if t.strip()]\n    return Bookmark(\n        id=row[\"id\"],\n        url=row[\"url\"],\n        title=row[\"title\"],\n        tags=tags,\n        created_at=row[\"created_at\"],\n        updated_at=row[\"updated_at\"],\n    )\n\n\ndef get_all_bookmarks(tag: Optional[str] = None) -> List[Bookmark]:\n    \"\"\"Return all bookmarks, optionally filtered by tag.\"\"\"\n    db = get_db()\n    if tag:\n        # Search for tag in the comma-separated tags column\n        rows = db.execute(\n            \"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE tags LIKE ? ORDER BY created_at DESC\",\n            (f\"%{tag}%\",),\n        ).fetchall()\n    else:\n        rows = db.execute(\n            \"SELECT * FROM bookmarks ORDER BY created_at DESC\"\n        ).fetchall()\n    return [_row_to_bookmark(r) for r in rows]\n\n\ndef get_bookmark(bookmark_id: int) -> Optional[Bookmark]:\n    \"\"\"Return a single bookmark by ID, or None if not found.\"\"\"\n    db = get_db()\n    row = db.execute(\n        \"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?\", (bookmark_id,)\n    ).fetchone()\n    if row is None:\n        return None\n    return _row_to_bookmark(row)\n\n\ndef create_bookmark(url: str, title: str, tags: List[str] = None) -> Bookmark:\n    \"\"\"Create a new bookmark and return it.\"\"\"\n    if tags is None:\n        tags = []\n    tags_str = \",\".join(tags)\n    db = get_db()\n    cursor = db.execute(\n        \"INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, tags) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\",\n        (url, title, tags_str),\n    )\n    db.commit()\n    return get_bookmark(cursor.lastrowid)\n\n\ndef update_bookmark(\n    bookmark_id: int,\n    url: Optional[str] = None,\n    title: Optional[str] = None,\n    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,\n) -> Optional[Bookmark]:\n    \"\"\"Update an existing bookmark. Only provided fields are changed.\"\"\"\n    existing = get_bookmark(bookmark_id)\n    if existing is None:\n        return None\n\n    new_url = url if url is not None else existing.url\n    new_title = title if title is not None else existing.title\n    new_tags = \",\".join(tags) if tags is not None else \",\".join(existing.tags)\n\n    db = get_db()\n    db.execute(\n        \"UPDATE bookmarks SET url = ?, title = ?, tags = ?, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?\",\n        (new_url, new_title, new_tags, bookmark_id),\n    )\n    db.commit()\n    return get_bookmark(bookmark_id)\n\n\ndef delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: int) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Delete a bookmark by ID. Returns True if deleted, False if not found.\"\"\"\n    db = get_db()\n    cursor = db.execute(\n        \"DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?\", (bookmark_id,)\n    )\n    db.commit()\n    return cursor.rowcount > 0\n"}<|call|>
